text: Gulf Security Monitor: Day 29 afternoon situation report — Iran's 3-state morning attack wave on Kuwait Airport, Salalah Port, and UAE industrial zone continues Iran's deliberate pattern of striking civilian infrastructure. Aircraft radar loss at Kuwait International Airport is the most operationally significant development — civil aviation safety throughout the Gulf region may now require rerouting. IATA has issued a notice to airlines operating in Persian Gulf airspace.
verificationNote: Kuwait Airport radar damage confirmed by Kuwait civil aviation authority. IATA notice consistent with prior Gulf airspace risk notices during conflict.

text: Islamabad Diplomacy Watch: Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan, Turkish FM Hakan Fidan, and Egyptian FM Badr Abdelatty have now all physically arrived in Islamabad — this is not a virtual call. Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar chairing the mediation. Key question: Can these nations deliver Iran's signature on a framework by April 6, Trump's new deadline? Iran submitted a written 5-point counter to the US 15-point plan — the exchange of written positions through Pakistan is the most structured diplomatic process since the war began.
verificationNote: Physical presence of all three FMs in Islamabad confirmed by Pakistan government and regional media. Iran's written counter-plan confirmed by PBS NewsHour and Al Jazeera.

text: Markets Pulse: Day 29 morning market summary — US equity futures flat as traders balance Islamabad summit hopes against continued strikes. Brent crude holding above $112 after closing at $112.57 on Day 28. WTI still near $100 — geopolitical premium holding despite Iran toll-booth allowing some transits. Key watch: if Houthis announce Bab al-Mandeb closure alongside Hormuz, Goldman projects +$25-40/bbl immediate spike. Michigan Consumer Sentiment at 53.3 — worst since 2022.
verificationNote: Brent/WTI prices per Bloomberg. Michigan Sentiment 53.3 per University of Michigan March preliminary. Goldman scenario analysis from published research notes.

text: Iran War Live: Day 29 full morning summary — (1) Houthis enter war: first ballistic missile at Israel intercepted; (2) Iran kills 2 journalists + 1 civilian in Lebanon morning strikes; (3) Gulf triple strike (Kuwait airport, Salalah port, UAE) pre-dawn; (4) Prince Sultan: 12 US wounded confirmed, AWACS damaged; (5) Islamabad summit underway — Saudi/Turkey/Egypt FMs physically present; (6) Hormuz blockade Day 26; (7) Oil $112/bbl, gas $3.98; (8) Interceptor stocks at critical low. Next key marker: April 6 Trump deadline.
verificationNote: Day 29 morning summary compiled from Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Washington Post, CBS News, and Jerusalem Post liveblogs.

text: Houthi Monitor: Houthi entry into the 2026 Iran war is their second major military campaign against Israel, following the 2023-24 Red Sea operations. AIS analysis shows no commercial traffic in Houthi-controlled Red Sea approach. If Bab al-Mandeb closes, it joins Hormuz to block two of world's five major shipping chokepoints — roughly 35% of global traded goods.
verificationNote: Houthi spokesman confirmed attack. AIS vessel tracking data public. Bab al-Mandeb threat via Houthi deputy information minister Mohammed Mansour confirmed by Haaretz, CNBC.

text: ISW analysis (Day 29): Houthi entry into the Iran war marks a significant expansion of the conflict's geographic scope. Houthi missiles likely sourced from pre-conflict Iranian stockpile transfers. The Bab al-Mandeb threat is credible — Houthis demonstrated capability during 2023-24 Red Sea campaign. Five simultaneous military fronts is unprecedented in post-WWII Middle East conflict.
verificationNote: ISW Critical Threats daily update assessment. CNBC confirmed Houthi entry. Five simultaneous fronts assessment is ISW analytical judgment.

text: OSINTdefender: Iran fired 7 separate missile salvos at Israel on Day 29 alone — a single-day record. Iron Dome tasked with unusual ballistic missile intercepts as Arrow stocks depleted. Open-source thermal imaging shows impact sites in Tel Aviv district (Neve Tzedek area), with Israeli rescue services at 11 confirmed locations. 1 civilian killed — a man in his 50s in central Tel Aviv.
verificationNote: Seven salvos confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. Impact sites geolocated by CNN. Casualty confirmed by Haaretz. Iron Dome ballistic intercept confirmed by RUSI/Haaretz.

text: War Monitor: Vance podcast signals a shift — he is clearly being positioned as the lead Iran negotiator ahead of Islamabad talks. Iran's preference for Vance over Kushner/Witkoff tracks with Tehran's desire for higher-level engagement. Fox News poll showing 64% disapproval of Trump's Iran war handling may be accelerating US internal timeline pressure.
verificationNote: Vance podcast confirmed by Benny Johnson show, reported by Haaretz and Times of Israel. Fox News poll confirmed by Fox News live updates. Iran preference for Vance confirmed via Axios.

text: Aurora Intelligence: Lebanon toll reaches 1,189 confirmed — 47 killed in 24 hours. Three journalists killed in a single strike on a marked media vehicle in Jezzine. WHO documents 9 paramedics killed in 5 separate strikes in one day — 51 total healthcare workers killed in Lebanon. Israeli anti-war protest in Tel Aviv with 1,200 attendees and 21 arrested is the largest single-location anti-war demonstration in Israel to date.
verificationNote: Lebanon toll confirmed by Lebanese Health Ministry. Three journalists confirmed by CPJ, Washington Post, PBS. WHO paramedic figures confirmed by WHO statement. Protest figures confirmed by Haaretz and CNN.

text: Tanker Trackers: Salalah Port suspended operations after Iranian drone destroys container crane. Maersk confirmed all crew safe; suspended ops for 48h for damage assessment. AIS shows 0 vessels actively transiting Hormuz in past 6 hours. Pakistan's 20-ship agreement (2/day) is marginal — would restore <0.5% of pre-war Hormuz flow. Brent at $107.81/bbl correctly pricing a prolonged disruption.
verificationNote: Salalah suspension confirmed by Maersk and Maritime Executive. Pakistan ship agreement confirmed by Washington Examiner and CNN liveblog. Brent price confirmed by Fortune/Bloomberg.

text: Iran War Live: Day 30 overnight — IDF launched 'widespread wave' of strikes on heart of Tehran: missile production facilities, command centers, naval weapons HQ all hit. Explosions heard across city; blasts in northern Tehran. Iran retaliates with cluster munitions over Tel Aviv — 8 impact sites, 1 civilian killed (60M), 2 injured. War enters its second month at maximum intensity on all fronts.
verificationNote: IDF Tehran strikes confirmed by Times of Israel Day 30 liveblog and Al Jazeera. Cluster munitions and Tel Aviv casualty confirmed by CNN and Times of Israel.

text: Conflict Monitor: Islamabad Day 2 begins with a tangible deliverable: Iran agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships (2/day) through Hormuz. This is the first crack in the blockade. Pakistan's FM Ishaq Dar confirmed the deal. It restores <0.5% of pre-war Hormuz flow but is symbolically significant — Iran signaling to international mediators that it can offer something. Germany's FM: direct US-Iran meeting 'very soon.' April 6 deadline looms.
verificationNote: Pakistani-flagged ship arrangement confirmed by Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar. German FM statement per Al Arabiya. Confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 30 liveblog.

text: ISW Day 30 assessment: At one month, Iran has fired 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones since Feb 28 — ~40% at Israel, ~60% at US targets. US can confirm only ~1/3 of Iran's missile arsenal destroyed; status of another 1/3 unknown. Iran may be launching small salvos throughout the day deliberately to keep Israeli civilians in perpetual shelter. 85% of Iran's surface-to-air missiles reportedly destroyed, exposing Iranian airspace to continued US/Israeli strikes.
verificationNote: ISW Critical Threats March 27 assessment. 85% SAM destruction per Israeli Army Radio cited by ISW. Missile/drone totals per ISW tracking of IRGC Aerospace Force waves.

text: Energy Intelligence Pro: Day 30 economics — Brent settled at $107.81/bbl as of Friday March 27; WTI $99.64/bbl. AAA national gas average hit $3.98/gal — up $1.00 in exactly one month (pre-war $2.98/AAA). Goldman Sachs estimates $14-18/bbl geopolitical risk premium currently baked in. Dubai physical crude trading at $126/bbl in spot markets. Trump's April 6 Hormuz deadline is the key price catalyst. If Iran holds, $130+ Brent scenario becomes consensus.
verificationNote: Brent/WTI prices per Fortune/CNBC March 27. AAA gas average per AAA Newsroom March 26. Goldman risk premium from published research. Dubai crude per Bloomberg physical markets.

text: Houthi Monitor: After firing two missiles at Israel on Day 29 (one ballistic, one cruise), Houthis have signaled they are in the war for the long term. Key risk: they have not yet executed on Bab al-Mandeb closure threat. If they act on that, combined Hormuz+Bab al-Mandeb blockade would be the worst peacetime maritime crisis since Suez 1956. 35% of global shipped goods would be disrupted simultaneously.
verificationNote: Second Houthi missile (cruise) confirmed by CNBC and Al Jazeera. Bab al-Mandeb threat per Houthi deputy minister. Trade volume figures from 2024 global shipping data.

text: Aurora Intelligence: Day 30 key data points as war enters month 2 — Iran 1,963+ killed, 24,910+ injured; Lebanon 1,189+ killed; US 13 KIA, 315+ WIA; Israel 28 killed. IDF conducted 8,500+ strikes in Iran; 400 Iranian ballistic missiles destroyed; 335 missile launchers eliminated. IRGC threat against US universities in Gulf (Texas A&M Qatar, Northwestern Qatar, NYU Abu Dhabi) is a new psychological escalation. IDF Sgt. Moshe Katz (22, New Haven CT) killed in south Lebanon — 7th IDF soldier killed in Lebanon since war began.
verificationNote: Death tolls from Al Jazeera tracker and Lebanese Health Ministry. IDF strike counts from IDF spokesman. University threat per NBC News liveblog. Katz death confirmed by Times of Israel Day 30 liveblog.

text: OSINTdefender: Open-source imagery from March 29 confirms total destruction of E-3G AWACS tail 81-0005 at Prince Sultan Air Base — fuselage split in two, rotodome displaced. This is the first US strategic command-and-control aircraft destroyed in combat since the Cold War. Also confirmed: 1 KC-135R completely destroyed, 3 heavily damaged. Iran's ability to hit aircraft at hardened NATO-grade facilities is a major intelligence success for the IRGC.
verificationNote: Imagery confirmed by Air & Space Forces Magazine and The War Zone. Tail number 81-0005 identified by aviation OSINT analysts.

text: Military Analysis Network: Pentagon ground ops planning for Iran (WaPo) is the most significant escalation signal since Day 1. Key data: Kharg Island handles ~80% of Iran's oil exports. A US seizure would be decisive economically but tactically complex — island is well-defended, shallow coastal waters favor Iranian mines. 82nd Airborne is the right tool for rapid-insertion raids but would need massive air cover. Iran's Parliament Speaker's response (threatening to 'fire and punish') signals they've read the same WaPo article.
verificationNote: WaPo ground ops report confirmed. Ghalibaf statement confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog. Kharg Island strategic importance per EIA petroleum data.

text: Gulf Security Intel: IRGC's targeting of Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) and EGA Abu Dhabi is strategically significant — both supply aluminium to Boeing, Lockheed, and BAE Systems supply chains. IRGC is specifically trying to create 'dual-use infrastructure' targeting precedents to justify civilian-sector strikes. Alba is 69.7% owned by the Bahraini government. EGA is 50% owned by the Abu Dhabi government. These are not random industrial targets — they are state-owned assets with defense sector exposure.
verificationNote: IRGC claim confirmed by Al Jazeera and CNBC. Alba and EGA ownership structure is public record per company filings.

text: Pakistan-Iran Diplomacy Monitor: Day 30 update on Islamabad summit — Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan, Turkish FM Hakan Fidan, and Egyptian FM Badr Abdelatty all present. Pakistan FM Dar confirmed 20 Pakistani-flagged ships approved for Hormuz transit (2/day). Two ships already crossed: MV Multan and P-Akili crude tanker (80M+ litres) en route to Karachi, docking March 31. This is operationally significant — the blockade's first actual crack. Iran appears to be using ship permissions as diplomatic currency ahead of any direct US-Iran meeting.
verificationNote: Summit confirmed by Al Jazeera. Ship names and cargo per Pakistan Today and Argus Media. Docking date per Pakistan Today.

text: Iran War Casualties Monitor: Updated Day 30 casualty summary — Iran confirmed killed: 1,963+ (Iran Health Ministry, Day 28); HRANA estimate: 3,202+ (Day 23). Lebanon: 1,189+ confirmed killed, 2,740+ wounded. Israel: 28 confirmed killed (7 IDF Lebanon + 21 civilians). US: 13 KIA, 315+ WIA. Gulf states: 27 confirmed, 282+ wounded (adding 4 from March 29 Bahrain/UAE aluminium attacks). France: 1 KIA, 7 WIA. Iraq: 83 PMF killed. Total confirmed war dead (all sides): 1,300+ in 30 days.
verificationNote: Figures compiled from Al Jazeera tracker, Lebanese Health Ministry, IDF, CENTCOM, HRANA, Lebanese Red Cross, and regional government reports.

text: Conflict Intelligence Network: Day 30 threat assessment — three converging developments raise escalation risk sharply: (1) Pentagon Kharg Island ground ops planning now public; (2) IRGC threatening US universities in Gulf with noon March 30 deadline; (3) Houthi missiles now reaching central Israel with debris casualties. Simultaneously, (4) Islamabad summit is the most structured ceasefire process yet; (5) IDF says days from completing all priority targets. Classic endgame pressure-diplomacy dynamic: maximum military threat meeting maximum diplomatic opportunity. Trump April 6 Hormuz deadline is the forcing function.
verificationNote: All component facts confirmed through Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, WaPo, and NBC News liveblogs.
